ctxMarket: Attention is focused on surging Solana network activity, with Q1 volumes exceeding $1.1 trillion, while price action has not matched on-chain growth. Security incidents and stablecoin management are also in the spotlight following high-profile protocol exploits.
ctxTalking:
• Solana’s network usage and whale on-chain flows have reached record highs
• Multiple DeFi hacks, including Drift Protocol, have triggered lawsuits and stablecoin pivots
• Wall Street-grade trading data is being introduced for Solana via DoubleZero Edge
ctxMatters: Rising network activity signals growing adoption and use cases on Solana, but security challenges remain prominent. The fallout from the Drift hack is reshaping stablecoin preferences and legal expectations for issuers. Enhanced trading infrastructure could attract more sophisticated participants to the Solana ecosystem.
ctxSignal:
Signal: Record Solana network activity and the shift from USDC to USDT in major protocols after exploits.
Noise: Speculation about immediate SOL price movements not directly tied to on-chain fundamentals.
ctxGlossary:
Drift Protocol — A DeFi platform on Solana recently exploited for $280 million.
USDC/USDT — Competing stablecoins used for transactions and reserves in DeFi.
DoubleZero Edge — A new high-speed data service delivering real-time Solana blockchain information.

readMore.title: Solana network activity explodes past $1.1T in Q1, but SOL is yet to catch up
At least a dozen crypto entities attacked since Drift Protocol hack
Circle hit with class action lawsuit over alleged inaction in $280 million Drift exploit
